message:
fix(lab): schema version in snapshot hash, spork drain-on-stop-failure, O(1) timer cancel

snapshot_restore: compute_hash now includes the schema_version in the
FNV-1a digest, so bumping the schema version invalidates all existing
snapshot hashes without needing to change the content encoding.
verify_integrity passes the stored schema_version to compute_hash.
Validation of open children now uses a pre-built HashSet of closed
region IDs instead of an O(n) linear scan per child. Test added for
schema version tampering detection.

spork_harness: stop_app now runs run_until_quiescent even if stop()
returns an error, honoring the structured-concurrency invariant that
all tasks must drain before the harness reports. The stop error is
still propagated to the caller after draining completes.

virtual_time_wheel: cancel() now inserts the timer_id into the
cancelled set without an O(n) heap search. Timer IDs are strictly
monotonic, so a stale handle's ID can never collide with a live timer.
next_deadline() eagerly pops cancelled entries from the heap front,
which amortizes the cleanup cost across advance_to calls instead of
paying it upfront.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
